title: Simulation Manifest
description: Defines an AWS incident simulation package for the play skill.

id.description: Unique sim ID. Format: 3-digit number + kebab-case slug. Example: 001-s3-bucket-breach

title.description: Human-readable title. Should include the fictional company name.

summary.description: Brief scenario summary shown in card tooltip.

services.description: AWS service slugs involved. Must match service column in learning/catalog.csv.

tags.description: Searchable tags for the scenario. Use kebab-case.

estimated_minutes.description: Estimated time to complete the simulation in minutes.

prerequisites.description: Sim IDs that should be completed before this one.

exam_topics.description: Exam topic references. Format: CertCode:DomainOrTopic. Example: SAA-C03:Domain1:SecureAccess

industry.description: Industry vertical (fintech, healthtech, e-commerce, media, logistics, etc.).

size.description: Brief company size description. Example: Series B startup, 45 engineers

glossary.description: AWS jargon definitions keyed by term. 5-10 entries per sim, 1-2 sentence definitions pitched at a beginner.

what_broke.description: One sentence summary of the failure mode. Shown only during resolution.

data_flow.description: Normal data flow through the system. Example: RDS -> ECS task -> S3 bucket -> Analytics vendor

name.description: Component name including resource name. Example: novapay-txn-rpts (S3 bucket)

role.description: What this component does in the system.

connections.description: How this component connects to others. Example: novapay-ecs-task-role writes via PutObject.

failure_impact.description: What happens if this component fails or is misconfigured.

components.description: Major system components with roles, connections, and failure impacts.

system.description: System context for the play agent. Helps the player build a mental model of the infrastructure.

service.description: AWS service slug this console represents.

artifacts.description: Relative paths to artifact files this console can serve.

capabilities.description: Simulated API actions this console supports. Keeps the play agent grounded in real AWS capabilities.

consoles.description: Service consoles the play agent can reference. Minimum 2 per sim. Every service in the top-level services array must have a matching console entry.

progressive_clues.description: Progressive clues from vaguest to most specific. Plain strings. The play agent reads them in order and decides when, if ever, to surface them based on the player's progress.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the player must do or identify.

required.description: Whether this criterion is required for successful resolution.

fix_criteria.description: Criteria for evaluating the player's resolution.

learning_objectives.description: Key takeaways the player should learn from this simulation.

sop_steps.description: How AWS recommends approaching this incident. Numbered remediation steps adapted to this sim's resources. Pre-computed from Agent SOP or best-practice fallback.

scenario.description: Alternative failure scenario for the same services.

how_it_differs.description: How this differs from the sim's actual root cause.

prevention.description: How to prevent this failure mode.

related_failure_modes.description: 2-4 alternative ways the same services could break. Shown during resolution debrief.

sop_practices.description: Best-practice takeaways from the AWS SOP, beyond the immediate fix. Preventive measures, guardrails, operational habits.
